title: trifolium ligusticum
intro: Trifolium ligusticum, also known as sweet clover, is a perennial legume native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its trifoliate leaves and fragrant flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to produce coumarin, a natural blood thinner that can help prevent blood clots and reduce inflammation. Consuming sweet clover has been associated with various health benefits, including lowering blood pressure, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting liver function. Trifolium ligusticum can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote overall well-being and alleviate specific health issues.
